description: CQC regulations for health and social care communications
disclaimer: This is a flag for review, not a compliance verdict. Consult a qualified compliance advisor for definitive guidance.

rules.rule_text: Claims about health outcomes must be evidence-based and not misleading.
rules.explanation_template: Claims like '{phrase}' about guaranteed health outcomes must be supported by robust evidence. Individual results vary.

rules.rule_text: Only registered healthcare professionals may provide medical advice.
rules.explanation_template: The phrase '{phrase}' may constitute medical advice. Only registered healthcare professionals should provide medical advice.

rules.rule_text: Patient information must be kept confidential and not disclosed without consent.
rules.explanation_template: References to '{phrase}' may involve patient data. Ensure compliance with confidentiality requirements and GDPR.

rules.rule_text: Claims about miracle cures or breakthrough treatments must be substantiated.
rules.explanation_template: Claims like '{phrase}' must be substantiated by peer-reviewed evidence. Avoid sensational language.

rules.rule_text: Claims about staff qualifications must be accurate and verifiable.
rules.explanation_template: Claims like '{phrase}' about staff must be accurate and verifiable. Maintain records of qualifications.

rules.rule_text: Safeguarding concerns must be handled according to proper protocols.
rules.explanation_template: References to '{phrase}' indicate safeguarding matters. Ensure proper protocols are followed and documented.

rules.rule_text: Consent must be obtained and documented for treatments and data sharing.
rules.explanation_template: References to '{phrase}' require careful consideration. Proper consent procedures must be followed and documented.

rules.rule_text: Claims about medication management must be accurate and within scope of practice.
rules.explanation_template: References to '{phrase}' indicate medication issues. Ensure proper incident reporting and documentation.

rules.rule_text: Communications must maintain dignity and respect for service users.
rules.explanation_template: Language like '{phrase}' may not reflect person-centred care principles. Use respectful, dignified language.

rules.rule_text: Claims of CQC registration must be accurate and up to date.
rules.explanation_template: Claims like '{phrase}' must be accurate and current. Registration status can be verified on the CQC website.

rules.rule_text: References to mental capacity must be handled sensitively and legally.
rules.explanation_template: References to '{phrase}' involve Mental Capacity Act requirements. Ensure proper assessment and documentation.

rules.rule_text: Deprivation of Liberty Safeguards (DoLS) references require proper authorization.
rules.explanation_template: References to '{phrase}' involve DoLS requirements. Ensure proper authorization and regular review.

rules.rule_text: Whistleblowing concerns must be handled according to proper procedures.
rules.explanation_template: References to '{phrase}' indicate concerns have been raised. Ensure proper whistleblowing procedures are followed.

rules.rule_text: Claims about infection control must be accurate and evidence-based.
rules.explanation_template: Claims like '{phrase}' must be supported by evidence. Infection control requires continuous monitoring.

rules.rule_text: End of life care communications must be sensitive and appropriate.
rules.explanation_template: References to '{phrase}' require sensitive handling. Ensure appropriate training and support for staff.

rules.rule_text: References to data handling must comply with GDPR and data protection laws.
rules.explanation_template: References to '{phrase}' involve data protection requirements. Ensure GDPR compliance and proper data sharing agreements.
